description: Enhance the universal tool registry with NLP-powered intent parsing, an AI-driven multi-step planner that handles natural language like 'schedule a meeting with the team tomorrow at 5pm and message them on chat', and a robust workflow executor with error handling, rollback support, and confirmation flow. Also add a voice-command-to-intent bridge in packages/ai that pipes speech transcription directly into the quant-tools orchestrator.
steps: Step 1: In packages/quant-tools/src/planner/intent-parser.ts, create a new IntentParser class that takes natural language input and produces structured intents. It should: (a) use regex + keyword patterns to detect multiple tool intents in a single sentence (e.g. 'schedule meeting AND message them'), (b) extract temporal expressions (tomorrow, at 5pm, next Monday) into ISO timestamps, (c) extract entity references (team, John, project-x) from input, (d) resolve pronouns/references ('them', 'it') using ContextManager. Export this from index.ts.

Step 2: In packages/quant-tools/src/planner/multi-step-planner.ts, extend MultiStepPlanner to add a method planFromParsedIntents(intents: ParsedIntent[], availableTools: ToolDefinition[]): ToolPlan that: (a) matches each parsed intent to the best-fit tool by combining tag/name/description scoring with the parsed action verb and entity types, (b) auto-wires dependencies (output of calendar.create feeds into chat.send as reference), (c) sets appropriate params from extracted entities/timestamps, (d) validates the resulting plan.

Step 3: In packages/quant-tools/src/executor/workflow-executor.ts, create a WorkflowExecutor class that wraps ToolExecutor with: (a) a confirmation gate - before executing tier>=2 steps, emit a 'confirmation_required' event and pause until confirmed, (b) rollback support - on failure, iterate completed steps in reverse and call their undoRecipe if available and not expired, (c) parallel execution of independent steps (no dependency between them), (d) timeout per step (configurable, default 30s), (e) retry with exponential backoff for transient failures (configurable max retries). Export from index.ts.

Step 4: In packages/quant-tools/src/orchestrator/cross-app-orchestrator.ts, update CrossAppOrchestrator to: (a) add a new method processNaturalLanguage(input: string, options: OrchestratorOptions): Promise<ToolResult[]> that uses the new IntentParser -> MultiStepPlanner.planFromParsedIntents -> WorkflowExecutor pipeline, (b) add event types 'confirmation_required', 'rollback_start', 'rollback_complete', 'step_retry' to OrchestratorEventType, (c) keep the existing execute() method working unchanged for backward compatibility.

Step 5: In packages/quant-tools/src/types.ts, add the new types: ParsedIntent { action: string; entities: Record<string, string>; temporal?: { startTime?: string; endTime?: string; duration?: number }; rawSegment: string; confidence: number }, WorkflowExecutionOptions extends ToolExecutionContext { confirmationCallback?: (step: ToolPlanStep) => Promise<boolean>; maxRetries?: number; stepTimeoutMs?: number; enableRollback?: boolean }, and WorkflowResult extends ToolResult { rollbackResults?: ToolResult[] }.

Step 6: In packages/ai/src/voice/voice-intent-bridge.ts, create a VoiceIntentBridge class that: (a) takes a SpeechToTextService and the CrossAppOrchestrator (from quant-tools), (b) has method processVoiceCommand(audio: Buffer, userId: string, sessionId: string): Promise<{transcript: string, plan: ToolPlan, results: ToolResult[]}> that transcribes audio then pipes the text into the orchestrator's processNaturalLanguage, (c) emits progress events so the UI can show real-time status. Add @quant/quant-tools as a dependency in packages/ai/package.json. Export from packages/ai/src/voice/index.ts and packages/ai/src/index.ts.
Step 7: Update packages/quant-tools/src/index.ts to export IntentParser, WorkflowExecutor, and new types. Update packages/quant-tools/src/orchestrator/index.ts to export new event types.

Step 8: In apps/quantai/src/app/api/ask/route.ts, update the POST handler to: (a) use the new processNaturalLanguage method instead of the old execute method, (b) add a new SSE event type 'confirmation_required' that the client can respond to, (c) add 'rollback_start'/'rollback_complete' event forwarding, (d) accept an optional 'voice' boolean field in the request body that, when true, expects base64-encoded audio in an 'audio' field and uses VoiceIntentBridge.

Step 9: In apps/quantai/src/components/AskQuantPanel.tsx, enhance to: (a) handle the new 'confirmation_required' SSE event by showing a confirmation dialog with the step details and sending a confirmation response, (b) handle 'rollback_start'/'rollback_complete' events to show rollback progress, (c) integrate VoiceToggle - when voice is active, record audio via MediaRecorder API, send as base64 to the /api/ask endpoint with voice:true, (d) show the parsed plan BEFORE execution starts (dry-run preview) with an 'Execute' button.
acceptance_criteria: IntentParser can parse 'schedule a meeting with the team tomorrow at 5pm and message them on chat' into 2 ParsedIntents with correct action verbs, entities, and temporal data
MultiStepPlanner.planFromParsedIntents produces a valid plan with auto-wired dependencies when given parsed intents for calendar+chat
WorkflowExecutor rolls back completed steps on failure using undoRecipe
WorkflowExecutor supports parallel execution of independent steps
CrossAppOrchestrator.processNaturalLanguage produces correct results for multi-app commands
VoiceIntentBridge correctly chains STT -> orchestrator and returns transcript + plan + results
The /api/ask route handles both text and voice inputs and streams SSE events including new event types
AskQuantPanel shows plan preview, handles confirmations, and integrates voice recording
All existing tests in quant-tools still pass (backward compatible)
findings: Pre-existing issue: @quant/database module not built causes tsconfig.backend.json typecheck failure and 4 backend test suites to fail (chat.service, session.service, agent-marketplace.service, tool.service). These are unaffected by our changes. The first tsconfig (main) passes cleanly for our code. All 73 quant-tools tests pass, all 193 ai tests pass, all 142 relevant quantai tests pass including ask-route.test.ts (4), ask-quant-panel.test.tsx (11), and tool-call-card.test.tsx (12). Added @quant/ai path mapping to apps/quantai/tsconfig.json to resolve import resolution for the route file.
